concept: Squeeze / Press Together / Compress in the Hand
description: To squeeze: the fruit for its juice, the sponge dry, a hand in reassurance. Pressure brought inward from both sides. With 'pesa' (push) and 'natu' (pull) it completes the hand's basic mechanics.
sound_symbolism: 'pe' presses, 'ki' is the tight point the pressing reaches — compression down to its focus.
grammatical_notes: Transitive: 'mia lureko peki.' (I squeeze the fruit.) Gently, of hands: 'shia mia thumai peki.' (They squeeze my finger... my hand.) For juice: 'lo mia lureko lila phialu peki.' (We squeeze fruit for its water.)

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The squeezed hand says what words sometimes cannot — pressure as tenderness, the gentlest use of force the body knows.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Juice, oil, whey, dye: the press was the quiet engine of the pantry, and the hand was the first press.
